title: Why would it benefit Shinji to see Gendous behaviors
display_title: Why would it benefit Shinji to see Gendous behaviors
wikitext:
[]: Why would it benefit Shinji to see Gendou's behaviors?             Quote Post by unitM » Mon Oct 20, 2014 11:19 pm Fuyutsuki expresses open frustration with Gendou's decision to not meet with Shinji. Fuyutsuki then states that he feels Shinji could benefit from seeing how Gendou lives. Fuyutsuki's words are emotionally-laced as well, which adds to this statement's credibility. But the question is, if we let ourselves be led through Fuyutsuki's logic, why would it be beneficial for Shinji to see Gendou's lifestyle? The answer isn't exactly drawn out for us.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Oct 20, 2014 11:58 pm Shinji makes mistakes and does foolish thing in Q because of his ignorance. Ignorance led him to leave the Wunder and to later on pull the spears. Granted, Fuyu knew about neither of these events when he said this, but it stands to reason that the elder figure that we see in Q knows how ignorance results in unintended consequences. If Shinji sees Gendo's lifestyle, perhaps he can understand why his father behaves in the way he does. If he can understand that... well, we'll never know, since it didn't happen.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Tue Oct 21, 2014 12:28 am But Gendou's lifestyle as a whole? That's the thing - I know where you're coming from, but something tells me that if I came home and found out my dad was invoking some voodoo magic, part of which involves him sacrificing his soul, I wouldn't exclaim out of pleasure "gee daddy, that's a mighty big plan to save mom you have there! Mind if I help?" Not to mention Shinji has a legitimate vendetta against his pops at this point. Despite Fuyutsuki being aware of all this, he still pops the statement "I disagree that your son won't benefit from seeing you like this."

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 21, 2014 12:49 am unitM wrote: I know where you're coming from, but something tells me that if I came home and found out my dad was invoking some voodoo magic, part of which involves him sacrificing his soul, I wouldn't exclaim out of pleasure "gee daddy, that's a mighty big plan to save mom you have there! Mind if I help?"

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Tue Oct 21, 2014 1:08 am In other translations that I've read from other dubs, Fuyutsuki is skeptical that it's better to keep Shinji in the dark until the Dead Sea Scrolls contract has been amended to suit Nerv's plan. Take into account that Fuyutsuki is adament that Gendo is willing to any price to see his wish fulfilled, including his very soul. Tell me, is there any benefit to be had when everything you are is just thrown away for one thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 21, 2014 1:29 am Depends on your personal cost/benefit analysis and how much pleasure you can see yourself deriving from exactly what it is that you want to give anything away for.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Tue Oct 21, 2014 1:31 am Some people are willing to sacrifice their entire existence, mind, body, and soul if to them it serves a higher purpose and calling, even if they themselves do not get to witness it. Putting it in motion is enough for some....

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Tue Oct 21, 2014 5:35 pm wiser3754 wrote: Tell me, is there any benefit to be had when everything you are is just thrown away for one thing?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Oct 22, 2014 3:08 am I think it's more that Gendo intends to be the one triggering and controlling Instrumentality (whatever it is in NTE), so it'll go the way he wants no matter what other people might think. Which is also what he intended to do in NGE, until Rei decided otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Oct 22, 2014 11:09 am ElMariachi wrote: I think it's more that Gendo intends to be the one triggering and controlling Instrumentality (whatever it is in NTE), so it'll go the way he wants no matter what other people might think. Which is also what he intended to do in NGE, until Rei decided otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Oct 23, 2014 1:22 am As far as I'm concerned, at this point there would be nothing to be gained by Shinji learning anything about Gendo. Nada. Rather, I think it may have been beneficial for all parties involved if the Commander could've taken the time to try and understand his son (preferably pre-timeskip). Not by having him followed by Section 2 agents or reading progress reports submitted by Misato/Ritsuko, but by getting off his ass and taking a second to consider a point of view other than his own. The problem is that the full extent of his curiosity regarding Shinji begins and ends with psych evaluations for the sole purpose of manipulating the kid into initiating impacts left and right. In any case, even if Gendo were to finally receive that lobotomy he so desperately requires and could then somehow muster up an iota of genuine concern for Shinji, it's way too late in the game to start now. He should've been making an honest effort about two and a half apocalypses ago (y'know back when it might've made a damn bit of a difference).

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 23, 2014 3:58 am BC Baron wrote: As far as I'm concerned, at this point there would be nothing to be gained by Shinji learning anything about Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Oct 23, 2014 1:17 pm Yup. After all the shenanigans that have gone down, I don't see Shinji as being willing to reconcile with Gendo after this.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Oct 23, 2014 1:36 pm But in Japan, respect for one's elders under any circumstances is deeply ingrained.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Oct 23, 2014 1:57 pm The real question should be “Why would Fuyutsuki think Shinji should benefit from seeing Gendo’s actions? Wouldn’t that potentially ruin Gendo’s plans, whatever they are?”

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 23, 2014 3:10 pm ^ Well, all that Gendo and Fuyu needed for their plan to succeed was that the two spears be removed, the DSS Choker will then take care of Kaworu and WILLE take care of shutting down EVA-13. Breaking Shinji to the point that he will be desperate enough to take the spears even if Kaworu tell him otherwise is a risky gambit, but having him on their side and manipulating Kaworu into sacrificing himself would be a much safer bet. Then I suppose that neo-NERV would had arranged to send a couple of NS to escort Shinji back to HQ once he ejects. Or maybe have him as a mole inside WILLE once they take him back, playing Misato's sensitive chord for him.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Oct 23, 2014 5:20 pm ^ I wouldn't assume that NERV is entierly done with Shinji just yet.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 24, 2014 2:38 am ^ I really don't see how neo-NERV could draw anything from Shinji anymore, even if her gets out of his funk. Besides that would be really ridiculous to have the hero manipulated into triggering the apocalypse for the third time in a row! Also, even in Gendo's initial plan 14 years ago, his role was limited to awake EVA-01, presumably Gendo would take the lead from here with the Key of N, and so far the plan seems to be similar, except that he replaced EVA-01 by EVA-13.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Mon Oct 27, 2014 6:10 pm ElMariachi wrote: ^ I really don't see how neo-NERV could draw anything from Shinji anymore, even if her gets out of his funk. Besides that would be really ridiculous to have the hero manipulated into triggering the apocalypse for the third time in a row! Also, even in Gendo's initial plan 14 years ago, his role was limited to awake EVA-01, presumably Gendo would take the lead from here with the Key of N, and so far the plan seems to be similar, except that he replaced EVA-01 by EVA-13.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Oct 28, 2014 2:53 pm wiser3754 wrote: How is Nerv able to enact final impact without the trigger?
